question.text: Income from commodity derivative trading that is classified as speculative income is treated as:

answer.text: Speculative business income — with restrictions on set-off against non-speculative income
Capital gains — taxed at special rates

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Speculative commodity trading income is classified as speculative business income. Speculative losses can only be set off against speculative profits, not other business income. **The Trap:** Not capital gains (derivatives are generally business income). Not tax-free or agricultural income. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers tax treatment of derivative income. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** If your commodity trading qualifies as hedging (documented hedge relationship with physical position), the income can be treated as normal business income — getting hedge classification is tax-advantageous.
